markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[No Hard Feelings](/Feats.aspx?ID=8779) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Prerequisites**
[Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777)

</column>

---

Despite the devastation your weapons tend to inflict upon their targets or the destruction you might unleash upon an area, your foes still manage to walk away at the end of a fight—at least sometimes. You can choose to add the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait to your ranged weapons, making the choice of whether to add the trait or not just before each Strike.
name: No Hard Feelings

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[That Was a Close One, Huh?](/Feats.aspx?ID=8780) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Frequency**
once per round

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777)

**Requirements**
Either you used Accidental Shot and hit your opponent with the Strike this turn, or you used [Lucky Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3273) since your last turn and the triggering attack missed you.

</column>

---

You laugh innocently about the inexplicable luck of your last shot hitting its mark or how close the attack that just missed you came to taking your head off, causing your foes to second guess whether it’s a good idea to oppose you in battle. Attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) either the foe you hit with Accidental Shot or the foe that missed you due to Lucky Escape. You use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to attempt the check to Demoralize.
name: That Was a Close One, Huh?
requirement_markdown: Either you used Accidental Shot and hit your opponent with the Strike this turn, or you used [Lucky Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=3273) since your last turn and the triggering attack missed you.

</traits>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[I Meant to Do That](/Feats.aspx?ID=8783) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777)

**Requirements**
Your previous action was a ranged weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that missed a foe within 60 feet.

</column>

---

Somehow your stray bullet causes an unintended reaction that creates a problem for your enemy: perhaps a ricochet knocks your foe’s weapon away or they stumble over stray debris in an attempt to dodge your bullets. Roll a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to attempt to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382), or [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) the foe you missed.
name: I Meant to Do That
requirement_markdown: Your previous action was a ranged weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) that missed a foe within 60 feet.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chain Reaction](/Feats.aspx?ID=8784) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 142

**Archetype** [Unexpected Sharpshooter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=370)

**Frequency**
once per 10 minutes

**Prerequisites**
[Unexpected Sharpshooter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8777)

</column>

---

You fire your gun once, creating a devastating and unpredictable chain of events; perhaps your bullet strikes exactly the right spot on a water tower, causing it to flood and incite a stampede of horses which knock over a lantern that sets a city on fire. Whatever the exact chain reaction, and no matter how improbable, the indiscriminate catastrophe creates significant challenges for your enemies across a wide area while leaving everyone else alone.

Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with a ranged weapon, and if you hit, you can make another Strike at a target within 30 feet of the first target. If you hit the second target, attempt to Strike a third target within 30 feet of the second target, and so on, continuing until you miss a target. You can cease the chain at any point; otherwise, it ends when you first miss an attack. However, you can’t target the same creature more than once; each time you move to a new target, it must be a target you haven’t made a Strike against yet during this particular use of Chain Reaction.

This damage is caused by some improbable set of events that injures one target after another, rather than from actually making several shots. Perhaps you shot at a tree branch that then fell on the targets, or one target’s scream caused another to accidentally set off their gun and shoot themselves in the foot. This means that only the first target suffers any special effects tied to the bullet (if it was magical ammunition, for example), but each target you hit still takes the same damage dealt by your Strike. Normally, all damage is of the same damage type as the initial bullet. However, the GM might choose to change the damage type based on their description of the chain reaction. For example, if one target was burned by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), that target might take fire damage instead of physical damage.
name: Chain Reaction

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Adaptive Vision](/Feats.aspx?ID=8785) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 200

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

After years of using black powder weaponry in dimly lit spaces, you’ve become accustomed to dazzling flashes of light and sudden shifts in illumination. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against visual effects. If you roll a success on a saving throw against a [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect, you get a critical success instead.
name: Adaptive Vision

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clan Pistol](/Feats.aspx?ID=8786) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 200

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

Raised in the ancient halls of Dongun Hold or the surrounding region, you display your lineage with a [clan pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=197), rather than a [clan dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=368). You get one clan pistol of your clan for free, as it was given to you at birth. This replaces your clan dagger. Selling this pistol is a terrible taboo and earns you the disdain of other dwarves. You are trained with the clan pistol. In addition, when your clan pistol is visible, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus on checks to [Gather Information](/Rules.aspx?ID=2636) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) when interacting with citizens of Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or their allies.
name: Clan Pistol

>
[Spark Fist](/Feats.aspx?ID=8791) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 200

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

You dust explosive black powder on your fist or glove before attacking, which combusts as you hit an opponent. The resulting pops of flame harm both you and your enemy. Until the end of your next turn, your fist loses the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait and deals an additional 1 fire damage on a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). Each time you succeed on a fist Strike, you take 1 fire damage. If your fist would deal more than one weapon damage die, the fire damage dealt on a successful Strike, to both you and your opponent, is equal to the number of weapon damage dice.
name: Spark Fist

<traits>

---

You’re used to fighting and hiding in a haze of smoke and flame. You automatically succeed at the DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to target a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) creature if that creature is concealed only by smoke. When you are concealed or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) in smoke, increase the DC of the flat check to target you to 6 if you’re concealed or 12 if you’re hidden.
name: Smoke Sight

    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crafter's Instinct](/Feats.aspx?ID=8794) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 201

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

**Trigger**
You misfire with a firearm or you roll a failure on a Strike with a ranged weapon.

</column>

---

You sense a minor flaw in the weapon as you fire it, and quickly adjust your aim on the fly to avoid the flaw. If the triggering Strike was a misfire, you get a normal failure instead. If the triggering Strike was a failure, it becomes a glancing blow that deals minimum damage for the Strike (adding any bonuses as normal but getting a result of 1 for all damage dice that would be rolled on a success). The glancing blow does not apply other effects that would normally happen only on a hit.
name: Crafter's Instinct

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scrutinizing Gaze](/Feats.aspx?ID=8796) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 201

**Access**
You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

</column>

---

Your family’s traditions of defending against Geb’s undead have granted you a sixth sense for detecting spirits, haunts, and other restless dead, no matter their form or the strength of their presence. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Sense Motive](/Rules.aspx?ID=2555) when trying to determine if a creature is possessed or under the influence of an effect that would make them [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks made to see through disguises worn by [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), and a +2 circumstance bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) haunts or undead within 30 feet of you.

If you aren’t using the Seek action or searching, the GM automatically rolls a secret check for you to notice [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) or undead within 30 feet anyway. This check doesn’t gain the usual +2 circumstance bonus, and instead takes a –2 circumstance penalty.
name: Scrutinizing Gaze

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Fools Aplenty](/Sources.aspx?ID=323)

**Requirements**
You are unarmored.

</column>

---

_Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._

You enter the stance of the implacable wombat, your reinforced backside ready to jostle and batter those who offend you. The only [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) you can make are wombat butt unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 bludgeoning damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [hampering](/Traits.aspx?ID=806), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.  

While you are in Wombat Stance, you can't be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) by enemies of your level or lower.

<title level="2">Wombat Style leads to...</title>
[Wombat Bastion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8799), [Wombat Burrow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8801)
name: Wombat Style

markdown:
**Source** [Fools Aplenty](/Sources.aspx?ID=323) --- _Nethys Note: This is a part of the April Fool's content from Paizo's blogs._ Using the power of punnery, you deliver a lovingly tailored joke at a creature within 60 feet. Attempt a skill check using the skill that would be used to identify the creature (e.g. [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) for a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600)); you can instead attempt a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check with a +2 circumstance bonus if the target is trained in that Lore or could be identified by that Lore when [Recalling Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). If you target a creature that isn’t a family member, this ability gains the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. The target is then immune to your Dad Jokes for 1 hour. If you spend two actions to use this ability by setting up an especially elaborate punchline, your target also takes 1d4 mental damage, plus 1d4 additional damage at 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. **Critical Success** The joke is so blindingly perfect-yet-awful that the target dissociates from reality. The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the end of your next turn. Double the mental damage dealt to the target.
**Success** The target is stupefied 1 until the end of your next turn or until the creature targets you with a [hostile effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251), whichever happens first. The target takes full mental damage.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected. Your allies who hear the joke are so embarrassed by it that they can’t treat you as an ally for any effects until the end of your next turn.
name: Dad Joke

    right="Feat 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Jalmeri Heavenseeker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8805) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Agents of Edgewatch</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #158: Sixty Feet Under](/Sources.aspx?ID=47) pg. 78

**Archetype** [Jalmeri Heavenseeker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=86)

**Access**
trained by a current or former member of a House of Perfection

</column>

---

Your skill and dedication have set you on a path to master the techniques of Jalmeray's greatest martial artists: victors of the Challenge of Sky and Heaven. You become trained in either [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44). You gain either the [Ki Rush](/Feats.aspx?ID=432) or [Ki Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=433) monk feat, which grants you a ki spell and a focus pool of 1 Focus Point that you can recover using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) activity as a monk does. If you already have both these feats, you can instead choose a single 1st-level monk feat.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Jalmeri heavenseeker archetype.
name: Jalmeri Heavenseeker Dedication

With a loud shout, you unleash your ki in a crackling shroud of thunder and lightning that engulfs your body. Until the end of your next turn, your unarmed attacks and weapons you wield that have the monk trait deal additional electricity damage equal to one-half your level and additional sonic damage equal to one-half your level.

 If you also know the [_ki blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=484) ki spell, you can choose to deal either electricity or sonic damage with the spell instead of force while this effect is active (_ki blast_ loses the [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) trait and gains the [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) trait instead). Any creature who successfully [Grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you or is successfully Grappled by you also takes this damage immediately after the Grapple check is resolved.
name: Heaven's Thunder

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

**Requirements**
You are in Sky and Heaven Stance.

</column>

---

You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) toward an opponent. At any point in the Leap, you can make a single Strike against a creature within your reach. At 12th level, if you hit with this Strike, instead of finishing your Leap, you can immediately attempt a second Leap from the space where you struck the target of your Strike. You can make a single Strike against a target within your reach during this second Leap as well. At 16th level, you can attempt a third Leap if your second Strike hits, and you can attack a target at any point during this third Leap. All Leaps made during this activity gain a +10-foot status bonus to the total height or distance of your leap, and no two Strikes can target the same creature.
name: Skyseeker
requirement_markdown: You are in Sky and Heaven Stance.

</title>

<traits>

You Stride up to your Speed and your ki enshrouds your limbs in a terrifying stormy energy, increasing the reach of your Sky and Heaven Stance unarmed attacks by 5 feet.

 At the end of your movement, make up to three Strikes against a target you can reach, each at a –2 penalty. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn' t increase until after you have made all your attacks. Instead of the usual slashing or piercing damage, the damage for these Strikes is either electricity or sonic, as you choose with each Strike. If you successfully hit with all three Strikes, the target is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 3.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 24

**Archetype** [Hellknight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=372)

**Prerequisites**
trained by a Hellknight Order

**Access**
You're from Old Cheliax.

</column>

---

You've started walking the path toward becoming a true Hellknight, though you're currently considered only an armiger. You've been taught how to terrorize others into compliance, but you also continually study the structure and hierarchy of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42). You must survive painful tests of your body and mind called reckonings that steady your willpower against all sorts of trauma. You gain resistance to mental damage equal to 1 + your number of archetype class feats from the Hellknight archetype. You become trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. 

In addition, you gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for Hell Lore; if you were already trained in Hell Lore, you also become trained in a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill of your choice.

<title level="2">Hellknight Dedication leads to...</title>
[Advanced Order Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=8827), [Ardent Armiger](/Feats.aspx?ID=8813), [Armiger's Protection](/Feats.aspx?ID=8814), [Diabolic Certitude](/Feats.aspx?ID=8815), [Hellknight Mobility](/Feats.aspx?ID=8820), [Hellknight Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8818), [Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819), [Hellknight-Errant](/Feats.aspx?ID=8817), [Mortification](/Feats.aspx?ID=8816), [Order Cross-Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=8830), [Order Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=8822), [Sense Iniquity](/Feats.aspx?ID=8823)
name: Hellknight Dedication

[Ardent Armiger](/Feats.aspx?ID=8813) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812)

</column>

---

You've trained to resist the manipulations of [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), and your Hellknight discipline makes you difficult to influence. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against effects that could inflict the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) or [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) conditions.

 If a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect would compel you to act in a way that violates your Hellknight order's tenets (as determined by the GM), you can attempt to break free from the effect as a free action triggered by receiving the violating order, and you gain a new Will save against the effect. You can attempt this save even if the effect would normally prevent you from acting of your own volition. You can attempt this new save only once for a given effect, even if you're compelled to violate your order's tenets multiple times. You can't receive a worse result on this Will save than you originally got against the compelling mental effect.
name: Ardent Armiger

You've learned to wear the iconic Hellknight armor. You become trained in light armor and [Hellknight breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=23), a medium armor. If you were already trained in light armor and medium armor, you gain training in [Hellknight half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=24) and [Hellknight plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=25). Whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency in any type of armor (but not unarmored defense), you also gain that proficiency in the armor types granted to you by this feat. If you have a class feature that grants you expert proficiency in unarmored defense and you're 13th level or higher, you also become an expert in the armor types granted to you by this feat.

 In addition, when you choose this feat, you receive a non-magical suit of Hellknight armor of a type you become trained in (Hellknight breastplate, Hellknight half plate, or Hellknight plate).
name: Armiger's Protection

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

Either through unfortunate circumstances or your own choice, you remain a Hellknight armiger instead of entering the true ranks of your order, though you still believe in the tenets of law and order. You likely take on an adventuring life with a group of like-minded individuals who aren't Hellknights. The resistance to mental damage you gained from the Hellknight Dedication feat increases to 5 + your number of archetype class feats from the Hellknight archetype.

 You also gain the Warding Shift action, which allows you to keep your allies from harm.

 **Warding Shift** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Requirements** You're adjacent to a willing ally; **Effect** You move the adjacent willing ally 5 feet in any direction and can Step into the space the ally vacated.

**Special** You can't take both this feat and [Hellknight Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8818) or [Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819).
name: Hellknight-Errant

</column>

---

You've forged your body to be able to stand toe-to-toe with a devil, and your mind has been shaped by the strictures of the Measure and the Chain. You've become a true Hellknight. You gain expert proficiency rank in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (or in another skill in which you're trained of your choice, if you were already an expert in Intimidation). You gain the armor specialization effects of [Hellknight breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=23), [Hellknight half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=24), and [Hellknight plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=25), and your resistance from that armor specialization is 1 higher than normal. In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Intimidation checks while wearing any Hellknight armor.

**Special** You can't take both this feat and [Hellknight-Errant](/Feats.aspx?ID=8817) or [Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819).

<title level="2">Hellknight Preferment leads to...</title>
[Blade of Law](/Feats.aspx?ID=8824), [Hell's Armaments](/Feats.aspx?ID=8829), [Infernal Interference](/Feats.aspx?ID=8833), [March of Law](/Feats.aspx?ID=8831)
name: Hellknight Preferment
prerequisite_markdown: [Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812); passed the Hellknight Test; trained in [Hellknight plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=25)

[Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

[Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812); passed the Hellknight Test; trained in [Hellknight breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=23); you have a spellcasting class feature.

</column>

---

You've bolstered your force of will with the power of the Measure and the Chain. You've officially joined the ranks of Hellknight signifers, and you receive a signifer's mask, often devoid of eyeholes or other decorative features. The mask doesn't obscure your vision, though it makes it impossible for others to see your eyes. While wearing your signifer's mask, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394), and Deception DCs against [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302). You gain expert proficiency rank in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) (or in another skill in which you're trained of your choice, if you were already an expert in Intimidation), as well as in your choice of [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46).

**Special** You can't take both this feat and [Hellknight-Errant](/Feats.aspx?ID=8817) or [Hellknight Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8818).

<title level="2">Hellknight Signifer Preferment leads to...</title>
[Branding Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8834), [Conjure Hell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8828), [Gaze of Veracity](/Feats.aspx?ID=8825), [Masked Casting](/Feats.aspx?ID=8821), [Signifer's Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8826)
name: Hellknight Signifer Preferment
prerequisite_markdown: [Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812); passed the Hellknight Test; trained in [Hellknight breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=23); you have a spellcasting class feature.

You're wearing your signifer's mask.

</column>

---

You harness the magical energy flowing through your eyeless mask to effortlessly protect you from [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effects. You [Avert your Gaze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2309). While Averting your Gaze in this way, you also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws and checks to disbelieve [illusions](/Traits.aspx?ID=629).
name: Masked Casting
requirement_markdown: You're wearing your signifer's mask.

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sense Iniquity](/Feats.aspx?ID=8823) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Hellknight Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8812)

</column>

---

Your Hellknight training gives you the ability to focus your senses and root out those who would attempt to deceive or hide from you. For 1 minute, you gain a +2 status bonus to your Perception DC against attempts to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387), [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) you, or [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to you. During this time, if you [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) to locate a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) creature or to see through a creature's [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) attempt, you gain a +2 status bonus to the Perception check.
name: Sense Iniquity

The Strike deals one extra weapon damage die and 1d8 spirit damage. At 18th level, this spirit damage increases to 2d8.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Signifer's Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8826) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

[Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819)

</column>

---

The magic flowing through your signifer's mask allows you to see in the dark, and the mask dampens the distraction caused by sudden flashes of bright light. While wearing your signifer's mask, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415); if you already have darkvision, you instead gain greater darkvision. Additionally, if you're [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) while wearing your signifer's mask and the target is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) only because you're dazzled, you reduce the flat check DC to target that creature from 5 to 3.

<title level="2">Signifer's Sight leads to...</title>
[Unfetter Sight](/Feats.aspx?ID=8832)
name: Signifer's Sight

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=737" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 26

**Archetype** [Hellknight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=372)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Hellknight Signifer Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8819)

</column>

---

Though you might not worship [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278) or other devils, you've studied enough of Hell to call forth a brief burst of its fire. Each creature in a 15-foot burst within 120 feet takes 9d6 fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save against your spell DC. This fire damage ignores an amount of fire resistance equal to your level, but not immunity to fire. A creature that fails its save also has its soul set aflame, taking 2d6 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (or 4d6 persistent spirit damage on a critical failure). At 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the initial fire damage increases by 2d6.
name: Conjure Hell

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 27 **Archetype** [Hellknight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=372) **Prerequisites** [Hellknight Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8818); expert in at least one of your [order's favored weapons](HellknightOrders.aspx) --- You've trained relentlessly with your order's weapon to maximize their deadliness. When you get a critical success on a Strike with one of your order's favored weapons, the target must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. If you have access to that weapon's [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203), you must choose whether to use that effect or this one. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until the end of your next turn.
**Failure** As success, and the target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) until the end of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** As success, and the target is slowed 2 until the end of your next turn.
name: Hell's Armaments
prerequisite_markdown: [Hellknight Preferment](/Feats.aspx?ID=8818); expert in at least one of your [order's favored weapons](HellknightOrders.aspx)

**Special** You can select this feat multiple times, gaining the lesser order benefit from a different order each time by training with members of that order.

When faced with lawbreakers, you become almost unstoppable, and your purposeful steps instill terror. Stride toward the required target with a +10-foot status bonus to your Speeds and ignoring [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=451). If you end your movement adjacent to the required target, each enemy adjacent to you who saw your movement becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). You can use March of Law while Burrowing, Climbing, Flying, or Swimming instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />

---

The flames of your magic mark a target as an enemy of order. If your next action is to Cast a non-cantrip Spell that deals fire damage, that spell brands the target with a mark only you can see if it fails its saving throw. This brand lasts for 1 week. For as long as the target is branded, you always know the general direction and approximate distance of the target's current location, as long as it's on the same plane. You also know the direction the branded target is currently moving, if any, as well as any conditions affecting it. You can have only one brand active at a time.
name: Branding Spell

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 32

**Archetype** [Golden Legionnaire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=373)

**Prerequisites**
trained in heavy armor

**Access**
You're invited by a current member of the Eagle Knights or the People's Council.

</column>

---

You're a wall of metal standing against the tide of Andoran's enemies. When you [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316), you also gain your shield's circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC against attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) you. You also gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for Warfare Lore. If you were already trained in Warfare Lore, you also become trained in a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill of your choice.

**Special** You can take this dedication even if you haven't taken more than two non-dedication feats from the [Eagle Knight archetype](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314).

<title level="2">Golden Legionnaire Dedication leads to...</title>

name: Golden Legionnaire Dedication

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 33 **Archetype** [Golden Legionnaire](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=373) **Prerequisites** [Golden Legionnaire Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8835) **Requirements** You aren't in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409). --- You take pride in the sheen of your armor, and how useful it is at distracting your enemies when you reflect light from it. Creatures within a 5-foot emanation that can see you must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC. Once a creature attempts a save against this effect, it's temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and dazzled for 2 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 2 rounds and dazzled for 1 minute.
name: Polished Distraction
requirement_markdown: You aren't in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Steel Falcon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8848) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 34

**Archetype** [Steel Falcon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=374)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39)

**Access**
You're invited by a current member of the Eagle Knights or the People's Council.

</column>

---

You serve as an envoy of the ideals of Andoran, whether it's in a foreign court or on the field of battle. Your training in the Steel Falcons enables you to anticipate the attitudes of others to achieve your goals. You gain the Size Up action.

 **Size Up** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) You designate a single, non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creature as your focus and assess its motivations. You must be able to see and hear the creature. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when you [Sense the Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) of your focus and a +2 circumstance bonus to your Perception DC should your focus attempt to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) against you. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against your focus. You can only have one creature designated as your focus at a time. If you use Size Up against a creature when you already have a creature designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new focus gains the designation. Your designation otherwise lasts until your next daily preparations.

**Special** You can take this dedication even if you haven't taken more than two non-dedication feats from the [Eagle Knight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314) archetype.

<title level="2">Steel Falcon Dedication leads to...</title>
[Denounce Tyranny](/Feats.aspx?ID=8849), [Frightening Indignation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8857), [Gray Corsair Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=8850), [Liberty's Promise](/Feats.aspx?ID=8851), [Perplex](/Feats.aspx?ID=8856), [Uncanny Prediction](/Feats.aspx?ID=8853)
name: Steel Falcon Dedication

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Denounce Tyranny](/Feats.aspx?ID=8849) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Linguistic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=642" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 35

**Archetype** [Steel Falcon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=374)

**Prerequisites**
[Steel Falcon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8848)

**Requirements**
You witnessed a creature harm an ally, harm a noncombatant, or commit a wantonly evil act in the last round.

</column>

---

You call out a creature's despicable or cowardly actions, disrupting its alliances or even making its allies ashamed to be seen with it. Attempt a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check and compare the result to the Will DCs of up to three of the triggering creature's allies within 30 feet of you. If you succeed on the check, the target refuses to treat the triggering creature as an ally for 1 round (2 rounds on a critical success). It's possible to get a different degree of success for each target. If you're a master in Diplomacy, you can instead target up to five creatures, and if you're legendary in Diplomacy, you can target up to 10 creatures.
name: Denounce Tyranny
requirement_markdown: You witnessed a creature harm an ally, harm a noncombatant, or commit a wantonly evil act in the last round.

</title>

<traits>

[Steel Falcon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8848); Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

</column>

---

Your devotion to Andoran's ideals is as much spiritual as philosophical. You gain the cleric's [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4644) feat but must select [freedom](/Domains.aspx?ID=77), [truth](/Domains.aspx?ID=95), or [zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=100) as your domain. You cast that domain's domain spell as a divine focus spell, and you [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) by contemplating or spreading the ideas of the Common Rule rather than praying to a god. When an ability would manifest a divine symbol—such as the [_word of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1842) focus spell—it instead manifests as an eagle clutching a sword in its talons.

**Special** You can take this feat multiple times, selecting a different domain each time.

<title level="2">Liberty's Promise leads to...</title>
[Liberty's Devotion](/Feats.aspx?ID=8855)
name: Liberty's Promise

You have an eerie ability to guess the next move of creatures you've Sized Up. You inform a foe that you know exactly what it'll do next, momentarily shocking it into inaction. Regardless of the result, the target becomes temporarily immune to your Uncanny Prediction for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 36

**Archetype** [Twilight Talon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=375)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

**Access**
You're invited by a current member of the Twilight Talons.

</column>

---

Few would even suspect that you're a member of the Eagle Knights, and you use the tricks of the spymaster's trade to keep it that way. You gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for Espionage Lore. If you were already trained in Espionage Lore, you also become trained in a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill of your choice. 

During your daily preparations, you can brush up on a specific field to gain the trained proficiency rank in one Lore skill of your choice. This proficiency lasts until you prepare again. Since this proficiency is temporary, you can't use it as a prerequisite for a skill increase or a permanent character option like a feat. If you have master proficiency in Espionage Lore, you gain expert proficiency in the chosen skill instead, and if you have legendary proficiency in Espionage Lore, you gain master proficiency in the skill.

**Special** You can take this dedication even if you haven't taken more than two non-dedication feats from the [Eagle Knight](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=314) archetype.

<title level="2">Twilight Talon Dedication leads to...</title>
[Burn Identity](/Feats.aspx?ID=8867), [Deceptive Deduction](/Feats.aspx?ID=8862), [Fabricated Credentials](/Feats.aspx?ID=8861), [Feign Innocence](/Feats.aspx?ID=8865), [Hidden Intentions](/Feats.aspx?ID=8863), [Make Do](/Feats.aspx?ID=8860), [Talon's Mark](/Feats.aspx?ID=8859)
name: Twilight Talon Dedication

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talon's Mark](/Feats.aspx?ID=8859) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 36

**Archetype** [Twilight Talon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=375)

**Prerequisites**
[Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858)

**Requirements**
You have a magical or mundane tattoo.

</column>

---

On the rare occasions that Twilight Talons need to identify themselves as members of the organization, many rely on an easily concealed tattoo to reveal their allegiance. Choose a tattoo you have. If the tattoo wasn't already a magic item, it becomes a level 6 item with the [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), and [tattoo](/Traits.aspx?ID=262) traits. It also gains the following abilities.

 **Activate—Twist Design** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) You transform your tattoo to a different form of approximately the same size. You can Dismiss this effect.

 **Activate—Adopt Persona** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Frequency once per day; Effect Your tattoo helps you create a temporary cover to infiltrate a protected area or escape a dangerous situation. It casts 2nd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) and 2nd-rank [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) on you. The duration of both effects is 10 minutes.

<title level="2">Talon's Mark leads to...</title>
[Inked Cache](/Feats.aspx?ID=8864), [Instant Credentials](/Feats.aspx?ID=8866)
name: Talon's Mark
requirement_markdown: You have a magical or mundane tattoo.

<traits>

[Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858); Master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

You're so familiar with the paperwork necessary to maintain your cover that you can easily whip up documents that support your fabrications. You can [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=2402) using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), rather than [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47), and doing so only takes 10 minutes, or 1 minute if you're legendary in Deception. With your paranoid nature, you're always on the lookout for forgeries. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) Perception or Society check for you any time you encounter a forgery, even if you don't examine it closely.

[Instant Credentials](/Feats.aspx?ID=8866)

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 37

**Prerequisites**
[Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858); Master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

You layer meanings in your words, and only those you've taken into your confidence can divine your true plans. You gain the [Doublespeak](/Feats.aspx?ID=6476) and [Slippery Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=5215) skill feats. On the first round of combat, if you roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) for initiative, creatures that haven't acted are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to you.
name: Hidden Intentions

</title>

---

You've learned to use your Twilight Talon's mark to secret away contraband. The tattoo's level increases to 10, unless it was already higher, and it gains the abilities of a [_greater retrieval belt_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3102). As a part of activating the tattoo to Store Item, you can attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against the Perception DCs of all creatures that are currently observing you. On a success, a creature doesn't notice you use Store Item.
name: Inked Cache

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 37 **Archetype** [Twilight Talon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=375) **Prerequisites** [Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858) --- You convince a creature that, despite appearances, you're on its side. Choose an enemy within 60 feet, and attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against its Will DC. The target is then temporarily immune to Feign Innocence for 1 hour. If you've taken a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against it this round, your result can be no better than a failure. **Critical Success** You and the target are considered allies until the end of your next turn or until you take a hostile action against it, whichever comes first. In order to take a hostile action against you, the target must succeed at a Will save against your Deception DC or it loses the action.
**Success** As critical success, but lasts until the end of your turn or until you take a hostile action against it, whichever comes first.
**Failure** No effect.
**Critical Failure** The target knows you attempted to fool it and plays along. You're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the target until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Feign Innocence

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

[Fabricated Credentials](/Feats.aspx?ID=8861); [Talon's Mark](/Feats.aspx?ID=8859)

</column>

---

In a pinch, ink from your tattoo arranges itself into a temporary pass, writ of authority, or similar document. If you're in possession of a blank page of paper, you can [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=2402) as a single action with the concentrate and occult traits. The forgery lasts for 1 minute, and during this time, you can't use any of the other abilities of your Talon's Mark. You can Dismiss the forgery, leaving the paper blank once again.
name: Instant Credentials

>
[Burn Identity](/Feats.aspx?ID=8867) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=710" />
</traits>

**Archetype** [Twilight Talon](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=375)

**Prerequisites**
[Twilight Talon Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8858)

</column>

---

You transport yourself up to 1 mile, as a 5th-rank [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) spell. Everyone who has seen you in the last 24 hours must succeed at a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, or forget your physical appearance as though affected by a 6th-rank [_rewrite memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) spell. You can choose to exempt any number of creatures from this effect. You can instead Burn Identity as an activity that takes 10 minutes. If you do, instead of the effects of translocate, you can instantly teleport yourself and up to four targets touched (either willing creatures or objects roughly the size of a creature) to the nearest border of Andoran, in addition to its other effects.
name: Burn Identity

You've spent so many hours at the Ivy Playhouse or sparring with the Street Performers and Actors' Guild that you've picked up some stage combat techniques. In a fight, you can apply these approaches to make an attack less harmful but more confounding to your foes. Make a melee Strike. If you hit, you take a circumstance penalty to damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice, and the damage gains the nonlethal trait. However, the target is perplexed by the theatricality of your attack and becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the end of your next turn.
name: Ivy Playhouse Strike

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

**Trigger**
An ally within 30 feet fails a Will saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect.

</column>

---

You expect the same level of urbane sophistication from your allies as you possess, so whenever one of them succumbs to egregious behavior from a mental effect, you're immediately mortified. How dare they make you look bad! Whether you respond with noisy contempt or quiet distress, your reaction gives your ally a second chance to shake off the effect and save the moment, at least socially. Your ally rerolls the triggering Will saving throw with a +1 circumstance bonus and uses the higher result. Regardless of the result of the save, your ally is temporarily immune to Such a Disgrace! for 10 minutes.
name: Such a Disgrace!

Whether in the salon or on the street, you've captured the attention of everyone around you. In this moment, they're all hanging on your next word or anticipating your next move. You capture the moment by striking an unforgettable pose, one that your friends find alluring and your foes find alarming. Roll a Diplomacy or Intimidation check. You can substitute the result of this check for one Reflex save you would be required to attempt in the next minute.

<traits>

---

Though you're hardly ready to retire from the social life, you appreciate the luxury the older wealthy elite enjoy within the private social club of Magpie Manor in Absalom's Petal District. And you try to bring that luxury even to the wilds of the world. If you have a four-person tent and spend at least 10 minutes setting it up to your level of comfort before taking an 8-hour period of rest, you regain an additional number Hit Points equal to double your level and reduce the severity of your [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition by 2 instead of 1. Up to three other Medium or smaller creatures can share the tent with you and gain these benefits.
name: Magpie Manor Recovery

>
[Bloom Cabaret Entrance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8874) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Incapacitation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=631" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 47

**Archetype** [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=246)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302)

**Trigger**
You’re about to roll initiative.

</column>

---

In socially exclusive venues like the Ivy District's Bloom Cabaret, you've learned how to make an entrance. This confidence doesn't betray you when you find yourself about to enter combat, allowing you to throw enemies temporarily off-balance. Once per day, you can choose to not roll initiative. Instead, you voluntarily go last. If more than one character uses this ability or another ability to go last, use the normal rules for resolving a tie: NPCs and monsters act before PCs, and within those groups, the creature can choose whichever order they want.

 Enemies who can see you must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. On a failure, the enemy is overcome with anticipation for what you'll do or say next and can't use reactions for 1 round.

 In addition, during your first turn, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the first Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation check you attempt.
name: Bloom Cabaret Entrance

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 47 **Archetype** [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=246) **Prerequisites** [Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302) --- Your history of coquetries, flirtations, dalliances, and liaisons is so lengthy that, even in a city as large as Absalom, you're bound to run into your past at inopportune moments. Fortunately, the individual involved might be just as shocked as you are by the unexpected encounter. Choose a target within 30 feet and spin a lascivious tale of how the two of you might have met before. Attempt a Deception check against the target's Will DC. Regardless of your result, the target is temporarily immune to your Flower Street Infamy for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 3 for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
**Failure** The target is stupefied 1 until the start of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected.
name: Flower Street Infamy

feat_markdown: Do You Know Who I Am?
markdown:
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 47 **Archetype** [Dandy](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=246) **Prerequisites** [Dandy Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6302); Master in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) --- Your reputation casts a long shadow, allowing you to throw your cultural weight around, especially in larger settlements. With a well-timed tirade or attention-getting shout, you can break a foe's mind and bring them low. Choose a foe within 30 feet, and attempt an Intimidation check against the target's Will DC. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this check if you're in a city or a +2 circumstance bonus to this check if you're in a metropolis. If you're specifically in Absalom, the bonus is instead +3. The target is then temporarily immune to your Do You Know Who I Am? for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target takes 12d6 mental damage and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3. It can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn adjacent to you.
**Success** The target takes 6d6 mental damage and becomes frightened 2.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected, and the embarrassment causes you to take 6d6 mental damage.
name: Do You Know Who I Am?

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

</column>

---

Divine glory awaits! You embrace your longing to make a true and lasting difference in the Universe by embarking on the path to apotheosis. You dedicate yourself to this journey, striving to overcome all your imperfections. Each day during your daily preparations, choose a skill you're untrained in. You can attempt skill actions that normally require you to be trained in that skill, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to checks with that skill until your next daily preparations. At 8th level, this bonus increases to +2, and at 15th level, this bonus increases to +3.

<title level="2">Starstone Aspirant Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aroden's Innovation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8884), [Cayden's Freedom](/Feats.aspx?ID=8881), [For the Fallen!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8878), [Gather Disciples](/Feats.aspx?ID=8885), [Iomedae's Valor](/Feats.aspx?ID=8882), [Minor Deific Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=8879), [Norgorber's Secret](/Feats.aspx?ID=8880)
name: Starstone Aspirant Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[For the Fallen!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8878) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Press" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=675" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877)

</column>

---

So many other hopefuls have attempted the Test of the _Starstone_ before you and failed. Whether they were killed or disappeared, you vow to honor their memories by succeeding where they failed. This drive allows to push forward after you've already made an attack. You Step and then Strike.
name: For the Fallen!

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Minor Deific Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=8879) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877)

</column>

---

Your mental, physical, and spiritual training have granted you access to divine magic that doesn't rely on any other deity or patron. Choose a common divine cantrip and one common 1st-rank [divine spell](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2). You can cast the cantrip as a divine innate spell at will and the 1st-rank spell as a divine innate spell once per day.

<title level="2">Minor Deific Power leads to...</title>
[Advanced Deific Power](/Feats.aspx?ID=8883)
name: Minor Deific Power

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Norgorber's Secret](/Feats.aspx?ID=8880) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877)

</column>

---

Being the god of secrets, Norgorber has erased all traces of his life before his ascension so that his every mortal act and even his identity remain unknown and unknowable. Something about this path intrigues you, and you've trained your own mind to defy intrusions. You gain mental resistance equal to half your level and a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects that attempt to read your mind or discern your location.
name: Norgorber's Secret

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cayden's Freedom](/Feats.aspx?ID=8881) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877)

</column>

---

Even if you've never downed a tankard of ale nor indulged in a night of rowdy revelry, you appreciate the dedication to freedom from Cayden Cailean and his worshippers. After much physical practice, you've become difficult to restrain, and if you're ever grabbed, you can access a little bit of the Accidental God's luck. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC when resisting an opponent's attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you or [Swallow you Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77). In addition, you gain the Liberate Self action. 

<document level="2" id="action-3759" />
name: Cayden's Freedom

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Iomedae's Valor](/Feats.aspx?ID=8882) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877)

**Trigger**
You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed.

</column>

---

Though you might not be walking the virtuous path of honor and justice most often associated with Iomedae, you have studied her mortal bravery and can emulate it when you're on the brink of defeat. You avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point. You also gain a number of temporary Hit Points equal to your level that last for 1 minute and a +1 status bonus to AC for 1 round.
name: Iomedae's Valor

You've studied his works, and you understand the need to constantly reinvent yourself to meet new challenges. Each day during your daily preparations, choose a general feat of 3rd level or lower. If you meet the feat's prerequisites, you gain the benefits of that feat until your next daily preparations.
name: Aroden's Innovation

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Gather Disciples](/Feats.aspx?ID=8885) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Starstone Aspirant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=376)

**Prerequisites**
[Starstone Aspirant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8877); Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)

</column>

---

Gods need followers, and you know that its best to gather a flock in the present to support you in your endeavor. Of course, you'll reward their faith in you in the future when you become a true deity. You can spend 1 week of downtime in a settlement to evangelize your coming godhood and establish a small group of disciples to your faith. Once this group has been established, these disciples are always helpful to you. You can use Religion to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) something of them, though your request must be able to be performed by the type of people who would follow you, at the GM's discretion. If you critically succeed at this check, your disciples give a piece of helpful advice or let you in on a small secret, granting you or a member of your party a +2 circumstance bonus to the first skill check you attempt when acting on the favor. If you critically fail this check, your disciples' attitude toward you doesn't worsen.

 You can have three groups of disciples at any one time (or five if you're legendary in Religion). If you exceed this number, the oldest group disbands and must be established again if you return to that settlement.
name: Gather Disciples

<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />

</column>

---

You grant greater healing when the gods don't interfere. When you roll a success to [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) for a creature that hasn't regained Hit Points from divine magic in the past 24 hours, you get a critical success on your check instead and restore the corresponding amount of Hit Points.
name: Mortal Healing
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42); you follow the Laws of Mortality

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paragon Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=8890) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 60

**Archetype** [Pure Legion Enforcer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=377) (Level 7)

**Prerequisites**
[Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=5125); Master in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42)

**Access**
You’re from Rahadoum.

</column>

---

You've learned the modern uses of Battle Medicine originated by Kassi Aziril. When you successfully use Battle Medicine, you can also reduce the target's [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) condition by 1 (this has no effect if you are subject to an effect continually applying the clumsy condition, like enlarge). If you are legendary in Medicine, you can choose to reduce the target's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) condition by 1 instead, and if you have the [Godless Healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=8888) feat, you can choose to reduce the target's [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) or [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) condition by 1 instead. If you have the [Mortal Healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=8889) feat, you can reduce all available conditions by 1 for a target who hasn't benefited from [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) or [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) magic in the last 24 hours, and if you roll a critical success before applying the effects of the feat, you reduce all available conditions by 2 for that target instead.
name: Paragon Battle Medicine

markdown:
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 60 **Archetype** [Pure Legion Enforcer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=377) **Prerequisites** member of the Pure Legion --- You are sworn to uphold the Laws of Mortality within the borders of Rahadoum. As part of your commissioning, you learned about the different types of worship and divine magic that you might encounter. You become trained in Intimidation and Religion. If you are already trained in one or both of these skills, you become an expert in that skill. If you are already an expert in both skills, you become trained in a skill of your choice. You also gain the Recognize Spell skill feat. Whenever you attempt to recognize a divine spell, you always learn it is divine and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the Religion skill check. Finally, you gain the following edicts and anathema. Knowingly committing acts that violate the anathema result in you losing access to abilities granted by this archetype, as determined by your GM. You can regain these lost abilities by spending an appropriate amount of time rededicating yourself to the Pure Legion, typically by donating an amount equal to 20 gp × your level to appropriate causes and eschewing all divine magic (including spells cast by others) for at least 1 day. **Edicts** confiscate, contain, or destroy all divine objects in Rahadoum; drive out divine casters from Rahadoum
**Anathema** use divine abilities, items, or spells; permit proselytizing of religion in Rahadoum; worship a deity Pure Legion Enforcer Dedication leads to... [Edict of Mortality](/Feats.aspx?ID=8892), [Mortal Possibility](/Feats.aspx?ID=8900), [No Gods, Only Mortals!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8894), [Repelling Enforcer](/Feats.aspx?ID=8899), [Shake Off the Gods](/Feats.aspx?ID=8896), [Throttle the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=8897), [Traces of the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=8895), [Unassailable Enforcer](/Feats.aspx?ID=8893), [Wrath Against the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=8898)
name: Pure Legion Enforcer Dedication

markdown:
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 61 **Archetype** [Pure Legion Enforcer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=377) **Prerequisites** [Pure Legion Enforcer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8891) --- You recite an excerpt of the Laws of Mortality, often the first law: “Let no mortal be beholden to a god.” Attempt an Intimidation check against all enemies within 60 feet that worship a deity, comparing your result against each target's Will DC. Targets are then temporarily immune to your use of this ability for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. Until the start of your next turn, if the target attempts to Cast a Spell with the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) trait, it must succeed at a DC 7 flat check or the action is lost.
**Success** As critical success, but frightened 1, and the DC of the flat check is 5.
name: Edict of Mortality

You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) and Survival checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) creatures that can cast divine spells. In addition, you automatically succeed at the flat check to target a [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) creature that can cast divine spells, and the flat check to target a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) creature that can cast divine spells is reduced to DC 5.

<traits>

You grasp the godly by their throats and damage their connection to their deities. When you have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), it must roll its flat check to use manipulate actions with the divine trait twice and take the worse result. Each time it attempts such an action while you have it grabbed, you tighten your grip further, dealing bludgeoning damage equal to 2d6 + your Strength modifier to that target.
name: Throttle the Divine

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Prerequisites**
[Pure Legion Enforcer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8891)

</column>

---

You channel your commitment to removing the divine from the world as you recite the Laws of Mortality. Strike a foe that can cast divine spells. The Strike deals two extra weapon damage dice. The target must also attempt a Will save against your class DC. On a failure, it loses its highest-rank available divine spell prepared or its highest unused spell slot to cast a divine spell. If it has multiple spells at its highest rank available, it randomly selects which spell is lost. On a critical failure, it also can't cast divine spells until the start of your next turn.
name: Wrath Against the Divine

The first time each day that you succeed at a skill check with that skill, you gain a +3 status bonus to your next skill check of any skill within the next hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brastlewark Snare Engineering](/Feats.aspx?ID=8901) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 72

**Archetype** [Snarecrafter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=265)

**Prerequisites**
[Snarecrafter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6414); [Snare Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=6506)

</column>

---

You've trained with the Brastlewark gnomes or studied their teachings, and learned the art of crafting their more magical snares. More information on magical snares can be found [here](Equipment.aspx?Category=31&Subcategory=120). You learn the formulas for crafting the _[pit illusion snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4343)_ and the _[shadow cloak snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4345)_ or two uncommon magical snares of your level or lower that you have access to. When you set a magical snare, the DC of any saving throw it requires uses your spell DC if that's higher. When you craft a non-magical snare, you can choose to give it the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) trait. When you do so and the snare is one that normally deals damage, it deals an additional amount of force damage equal to its level.

<title level="2">Brastlewark Snare Engineering leads to...</title>
[Domino Effect](/Feats.aspx?ID=8905), [Remote Detonation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8903)
name: Brastlewark Snare Engineering

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Barricade Snare](/Feats.aspx?ID=8902) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 72

**Archetype** [Snarecrafter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=265)

**Prerequisites**
[Snarecrafter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6414)

</column>

---

Using clever gnomish engineering, you can quickly erect small barriers in which your snares are hidden. When you prepare snares for quick deployment, you can prepare some of them as barricade snares. A barricade snare consists of a small barricade in which the snare is hidden, but it costs two of your quick deployment snares. Though creatures can automatically see the barricade, the snare hidden inside the barricade must be detected like a standard snare, but with a +2 circumstance bonus to the DC. The barricade occupies the same space as the snare and is 5 feet high; it provides standard cover, can be Climbed with a successful DC 12 Athletics check, has an AC of 10, Hardness 5, and 20 Hit Points, and is immune to critical hits and precision damage. The barricade lasts as long as the snare does. A creature triggers and is affected by the hidden snare when it attempts to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the barricade or successfully attacks the barricade while adjacent to it. The snare and barricade are destroyed when the snare is triggered or if the barricade is destroyed, whichever comes first.

**Special** Even if you have the Surprise Snare feat, you must craft a barricade snare in an empty square.
name: Barricade Snare

[Remote Detonation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8903) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />

</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Brastlewark Snare Engineering](/Feats.aspx?ID=8901)

</column>

---

Brastlewark snarecrafters know that sometimes it's more advantageous to magically destroy a snare before it's triggered. Choose a magical snare within 120 feet that you crafted. The snare is automatically destroyed and each creature in a 5-foot emanation takes 1d6 force damage per level of the destroyed snare with a basic Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC.

**Special** If you detonate a broad snare or giant snare, choose one square that snare occupies to be the center of the emanation.
name: Remote Detonation

You can create longer snares for quick deployment, creating lines on the battlefield. When you prepare snares for quick deployment, you can prepare some of them as broad snares. A broad snare takes up a 20-foot line starting from the square in which you craft the snare but it costs two of your quick deployment snares. A broad snare can trigger from a creature entering any portion of its area, and all its effects apply over the full area.
name: Broad Snare

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rose Warden Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8906) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 84

**Archetype** [Rose Warden](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=378)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48); you worship [Milani](/Deities.aspx?ID=352)

</column>

---

As a follower of the Everbloom, you stand up against tyranny and oppression, understanding that revolution against an unjust ruler must sometimes be enacted in secret. You become trained in Deception. If you're already trained in Deception, you become an expert in your choice of Deception or Stealth. When you get a critical success to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to a figure of authority, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC and saving throws against being [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by that figure of authority for 24 hours.

 You add the edicts and anathema of Milani to your own (see sidebar). Failing to uphold edicts or committing acts that violate the anathema result in you losing access to any abilities granted by this archetype, as determined by your GM. You can regain these lost abilities through the atone ritual as normal.

<title level="2">Rose Warden Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blessed Thorn Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8907), [Holy Bloom](/Feats.aspx?ID=8908), [Liberated Mind](/Feats.aspx?ID=8911), [Rise Up!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8914), [Unbalancing Appearance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8909), [Unfettering Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8910), [Wall of Roses](/Feats.aspx?ID=8912)
name: Rose Warden Dedication

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />

</column>

---

When you see a foe hurt one of your allies, your righteous retribution causes your weapon to sprout holy thorns. Make a Strike against the triggering enemy. If you hit and deal damage, the target must also succeed at a Fortitude saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher, or become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (or enfeebled 2 on a critical failure).
name: Blessed Thorn Strike
requirement_markdown: You witnessed an enemy harm an ally with a Strike or spell last round.

    pfs="Standard"

[Holy Bloom](/Feats.aspx?ID=8908) <actions string="" />
</title>
