---

Though you might not be a priest of Milani, her divine spirit flows through you. You gain holy sanctification. Select the [freedom](/Domains.aspx?ID=77) or [zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=100) domain. You gain the initial domain spell for that domain. If you don't have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) by praying to Milani or by working with others to plan a just uprising. You become trained in spell attack modifier and spell DC, and your spellcasting attribute for these spells is Wisdom.

**Special** You can choose this feat a second time, gaining the initial domain spell of the domain you didn't gain the first time.

<title level="2">Holy Bloom leads to...</title>
[Holy Flower](/Feats.aspx?ID=8913)

</column>

---

With a damaging blow, you cause a foe to loosen their grip on an ally they're holding down. Make a melee Strike against the creature. If you hit and deal damage, your ally can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) from being grabbed or restrained as a free action. If your Strike is a critical hit, your ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the attempt.

requirement_markdown: A creature within your reach has an ally [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90).

</title>

</column>

---

Roses are the symbol of Milani, and you can call forth a wall of them to stymie those who don't follow her teachings. You can cast [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) once per day as a 4th-rank divine innate spell. The wall is immune to fire and the piercing damage its thorns deal counts as silver for the purposes of resistances. Followers of Milani don't treat the wall's spaces as difficult terrain and don't take damage when they enter one of the wall's spaces.
name: Wall of Roses

You tense your muscles to make yourself neutrally buoyant and more difficult to move. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to force you to move. If any effect would force you to move 10 feet or more, attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a success, you move only 5 feet. Additionally, you don't have to take a [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) action each turn to avoid sinking or moving with the current.

<title level="2">Anchor Stance leads to...</title>
[Riptide](/Feats.aspx?ID=8923)

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 97 **Archetype** [Undersea Privateer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=379) **Prerequisites** [Anchor Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8916) **Requirements** You're in Anchor Stance, and your target is within 5 feet of the edge of a water vehicle or already in the water. --- You swirl water around you to make a current that pulls creatures closer. Attempt an Athletics check against the Fortitude DC of a creature within 20 feet. If the creature is willing, you increase your degree of success by one (turning a failure into a success and a success into a critical success). **Critical Success** The target is pulled 10 feet toward you.
**Success** The target is pulled 5 feet toward you.
**Critical Failure** Your movements cause you to be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Riptide
requirement_markdown: You're in Anchor Stance, and your target is within 5 feet of the edge of a water vehicle or already in the water.

markdown:
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 97 **Archetype** [Undersea Privateer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=379) **Prerequisites** [Undersea Privateer Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8915) --- Like a flying fish, you can build up momentum and launch yourself into the air. [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) toward the surface at up to twice your swim Speed, then attempt a DC 30 Athletics check to propel yourself out of the water. If you do not swim at least 10 feet, you automatically fail. If your [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) takes you near any structure or object and have at least one hand free, you can use the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) reaction. If you do not succeed in Grabbing an Edge, you do not take any falling damage as you re-enter the water but are submerged to a distance that equals the height of your Leap. **Critical Success** You Leap up to 15 feet vertically and 5 feet horizontally.
**Success** You Leap up to 10 feet vertically and 5 feet horizontally.
**Failure** You Leap up to 5 feet vertically and 5 feet horizontally.
**Critical Failure** You're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn. Propelled Leap leads to... [Sailfish Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8927)
name: Propelled Leap

You've learned how to convert your upward momentum into a powerful attack. At the top of your Propelled Leap, you can make a melee or ranged Strike as a reaction instead of choosing to [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313). Your Strike gains a +4 circumstance bonus to damage. After your attack, hit or miss, you fall back into the water.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sister of the Golden Erinys Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8929) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 109

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This feat does not allow Pathfinder Society members to become unholy or add the unholy trait to their weapons._

**Archetype** [Sister of the Golden Erinys](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=380)

**Prerequisites**
You're from Isger or a member of the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinys.

**Access**
You're from Isger or a member of the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinys.

</column>

---

The fighting style of the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinys is rooted in their understanding of devils. You gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for Devil Lore. When you choose this feat, you can choose to become sanctified as unholy. You gain access to the asp coil. You have familiarity with the [asp coil](/Weapons.aspx?ID=233) and [scourge](/Weapons.aspx?ID=531), treating them as simple weapons for the purposes of proficiency. If you're unholy, your Strikes using those weapons and your unarmed Strikes gain the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.

**Special** If you have the [Monastic Weaponry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5979) feat, the asp coil gains the monk trait for you.

<title level="2">Sister of the Golden Erinys Dedication leads to...</title>
[Eye for Weakness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8931), [Fiendish Brand](/Feats.aspx?ID=8932), [Golden Erinys Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8930), [Promise of Pain](/Feats.aspx?ID=8935), [Vengeance Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8933), [Wrathful Presence](/Feats.aspx?ID=8936)
name: Sister of the Golden Erinys Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: You're from Isger or a member of the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinys.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Golden Erinys Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=8930) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=701" />

**Prerequisites**
[Sister of the Golden Erinys Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8929)

</column>

---

Whether you're a keen student of vengeance or you picked up techniques after being a common target for the sisters' punishment, you've learned to dig painfully into your opponent's flesh. In this stance, you can make fury's fang unarmed attacks. These deal 1d6 piercing damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits.

 While in the stance, if you critically hit with a melee Strike that deals piercing damage, the target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (with a DC to remove equal to your class DC); this is in addition to any critical specialization effect you might gain from the attack.

<title level="2">Golden Erinys Stance leads to...</title>
[Cruel Piercing](/Feats.aspx?ID=8934)
name: Golden Erinys Stance

markdown:
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 120 **Archetype** [Nidalese Horselord](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=381) **Prerequisites** Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43); [horse](/Companions.aspx?ID=76) animal companion **Access** You’re from Nidal. --- Thanks to the deep bond you share with your Nidalese horse, the two of you gain remarkable abilities. You both gain darkvision and a +1 status bonus to saving throws against effects with the [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) or [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) trait. Your horse's Speed also increases by 5 feet. You gain the following edicts and anathema. **Edicts** oppose the forces of the Umbral Court where possible, provide comfort to those who suffer in darkness
**Anathema** mistreat your horse, worship Zon-Kuthon or permanently ally with those who worship Zon-Kuthon **Special** You can pledge yourself to the cause of the Nidalese horselords if you've already taken the [Cavalier Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=6286) feat, allowing you to take this dedication feat even if you haven't taken two additional cavalier feats. Nidalese Horselord Dedication leads to... [Defy Chains](/Feats.aspx?ID=8946), [Dismounting Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8942), [Face Your Fears](/Feats.aspx?ID=8938), [Fear No Pain!](/Feats.aspx?ID=8940), [Gallop Across Shadow](/Feats.aspx?ID=8947), [Horselord's Bond](/Feats.aspx?ID=8944), [Repel Darkness](/Feats.aspx?ID=8939), [Shadowpiercing Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=8945), [Swallow Pain](/Feats.aspx?ID=8943), [Swift Support](/Feats.aspx?ID=8941)
name: Nidalese Horselord Dedication

A lifetime of facing Nidal's terrors has taught you to fight through fear. Although you aren't immune to it, you can subdue the emotion to such an extent that many believe it doesn't affect you. Reduce the value of your [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition by 1. If this would reduce the value to 0, you are no longer frightened and gain a +1 circumstance bonus against fear effects for 1 minute.
name: Face Your Fears

Your stoicism and courage seem to drive off darkness and shadow. For 1 minute, you suppress magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) effects of a rank equal to or lower than half your level rounded up within a 10-foot emanation. This doesn't provide light, but merely restores the area to its natural illumination level. You can Dismiss this aura.

 If your horse is at least a mature animal companion, you can use Repel Darkness to cause the aura to emanate from your horse instead of you, as long as your horse is within 100 feet.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dismounting Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=8942) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

[Nidalese Horselord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8937)

**Requirements**
You are mounted on your horse, and your last action was to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) to order your horse to Stride at least once.

</column>

---

You leap from your horse, using the momentum of its movement to deal a devastating blow with your melee weapon. You use the [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) action to dismount your horse but can move to any space within 10 feet of your mount, as long as your horse moved at least 15 feet. You then make a melee Strike. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, you deal an extra die of weapon damage. If you're at least 14th level, increase this to two extra dice.
name: Dismounting Strike
requirement_markdown: You are mounted on your horse, and your last action was to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) to order your horse to Stride at least once.

You can make a melee Strike at up to two enemies that are within your reach at any point during your mount's movement. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both of them.

 Each Strike that hits a creature in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401) or darkness deals an additional 3d6 damage of the same type as the Strike. This additional damage increases to 4d6 at 15th level and to 5d6 at 18th level.

---

 In addition, if the fiend has an aura and you're in it, you can attempt an Intimidation check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the aura's effect, using half your level rounded up as the counteract rank. If you succeed, the effects of the aura are suppressed until the end of the triggering fiend's next turn.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />

[Nidalese Horselord Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8937)

</column>

---

Though your enemy wields shadow and darkness as weapons, they are no impediment to you when you ride your trusted horse. To that end, your mount can step onto patches of darkness as if they were solid. When your horse moves through an area of [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), it can treat empty spaces as solid ground. This allows your horse to move across gaps bridged by darkness or even into the air, ascending or descending at a maximum of a 45-degree angle. If your horse isn't on actual solid ground and the area your horse is in becomes [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2401) or brighter, your horse falls.
name: Gallop Across Shadow

You can craft tattoos, including magical tattoos. When you select this feat, you gain the formulas for four common magical tattoos of 2nd level or lower. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to Craft tattoos. If you’re a master in Crafting, this bonus increases to +2 and you gain the formulas for four common magical tattoos of 7th level or lower

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs=""
>
[Ursine Avenger Form](/Feats.aspx?ID=8950) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 183

**Archetype** [Ursine Avenger Hood](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=382)

</column>

---

You draw the [_Ursine Avenger Hood_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2368) over your head and its fur over your arms, assuming an ursine form that has traits of your original form as well as that of a [bear](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370). In your ursine form, you gain a [jaws ](/Weapons.aspx?ID=356) [unarmed attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=1584) that deals 1d8 piercing damage and a claws unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait. Both unarmed attacks are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group. You lose the ability to speak complex sentences while transformed and can only communicate through grunts and gestures; this prevents you from using effects that require a shared or spoken language until you revert back to your non-hybrid form. You can use this action while transformed to remove the hood and return to your original form.

<title level="2">Ursine Avenger Form leads to...</title>
[Fearsome Fangs](/Feats.aspx?ID=8957), [Great Bear](/Feats.aspx?ID=8955), [Immortal Bear](/Feats.aspx?ID=8959), [Terrible Transformation](/Feats.aspx?ID=8956)
name: Ursine Avenger Form

Your eyes are accustomed to the harsh glare of the sun on snow and ice. You gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against effects that inflict the [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) condition. Snow doesn’t impair your vision; you ignore [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424) from snowfall. Your skin becomes cold to the touch, and sometimes frost forms on you. You are protected from [severe cold and heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=1901).
name: Winter's Embrace

    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Quick Setup](/Feats.aspx?ID=8970) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 159

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Quick Setup feat is not permitted in Pathfinder Society play._

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

You have learned to setup simple crafting jobs with ease. Your setup time for consumables that are at least 6 levels lower than you is 1 day, and your setup time for permanent items 6 levels lower than you is 3 days.

If you rush the setup and reduce the setup time for consumables at least 6 levels lower than you to less than 1 day, you can instead set them up in just 2 hours (allowing you to do so during an adventuring day if you have access to your tools and materials). If you rush the setup of a permanent item at least 6 levels lower than you and reduce the setup time to less than 1 day, you can perform the setup in 4 hours.
name: Quick Setup

You’re an aristocrat or honored retainer from a Chelaxian noble house.

</column>

---

A noble house has invested in your training, imparting special techniques the family has honed over generations. You gain [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) as a skill feat, choosing from among Cheliax Lore, Devil Lore, Hell Lore, or a Lore associated with your house. In addition, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks, and your Will DC against Chelaxian citizens and [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389).

<title level="2">Chelaxian Scion Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aristocratic Arms](/Feats.aspx?ID=8973), [Patronize the Arts](/Feats.aspx?ID=8975), [Stoic as Hell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8976)
name: Chelaxian Scion Dedication

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 223 **Archetype** [Chelaxian Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=384) **Prerequisites** [Chelaxian Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8971) --- Through consistent (and often punishing) practice, you’ve learned how to use your house’s preferred weapons. You have familiarity with either one martial weapon of your choice; two simple or martial weapons of your choice from your house’s preferred weapons list; or one advanced weapon of your choice from your house’s preferred weapons list. For the purpose of proficiency, treat any of these selections that are martial weapons as simple weapons and any that are advanced weapons as martial weapons. If you’re at least an expert in one of the selected weapons, you get the critical specialization effect of that weapon when you get a critical hit with it. **Charthagnion**: Weapons with the [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577) or [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667) trait
**Elliendo**: [Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) weapons and [knives](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)
**Henderthane**: [Hammers](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8) and [picks](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10)
**Jeggare**: One-handed [swords](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15)
**Leroung**: [Clubs](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) and [shields](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12)
**Narikopolus**: [Bows](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
**Sarini**: [Flails](/Weapons.aspx?ID=374)
**Thrune**: [Clubs](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) and [crossbows](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17) In addition, you gain the For My House! action. **For My House!** **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You announce your house affiliation, either by declaring it aloud (giving this ability the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait) or openly displaying your house heraldry (giving this ability the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait). Until the beginning of your next turn, your weapon Strikes and spells deal an additional 1 damage to any creature that witnessed the display. If you’re an expert in [Cheliax Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Genealogy Lore, Heraldry Lore, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45), or Warfare Lore, the additional damage increases to 2. The damage increases to 3 if you’re a master in the skill or 4 if you’re legendary in the skill.
name: Aristocratic Arms

    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs=""
>
[Patronize the Arts](/Feats.aspx?ID=8975) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 223

**Archetype** [Chelaxian Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=384)

**Prerequisites**
[Chelaxian Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8971)

**Trigger**
An ally within 30 feet of you critically succeeds at an attack roll or skill check.

</column>

---

A true aristocrat knows how to encourage their best hirelings, ensuring loyalty and future excellence. You praise the triggering ally, granting them one of the following benefits. The ally is then immune to this ability for 1 day. 

**Eagerness:** Invigorated by praise, the ally can [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434), [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) with a nearby object, [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [Reload](/Rules.aspx?ID=2196), or [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) as a reaction. 

**Persistence:** For 1 minute, the ally gains [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half your level plus your Charisma modifier. 

**Validation:** If the ally has the [panache class feature](/Classes.aspx?ID=63), they gain panache.
name: Patronize the Arts

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Stoic as Hell](/Feats.aspx?ID=8976) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 223

**Archetype** [Chelaxian Scion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=384)

**Frequency**
once per minute

**Prerequisites**
[Chelaxian Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8971); Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); expert in Will

**Trigger**
You’re targeted by an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effect or effect that would give you the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition.

</column>

---

Like the [archdevil](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=4) [Dispater](/Deities.aspx?ID=397), you maintain an unshakable poise even under impossible stress. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the triggering saving throw or to your Will DC against the triggering effect. If you critically succeed at the saving throw (or if the source of the effect critically fails its skill check, such as to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) you), you gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half your level until the end of the effect’s source’s next turn. If the effect originated from a creature, you can Demoralize that creature once as a free action on your next turn; if the creature was temporarily immune to your attempts to Demoralize it, it loses that immunity.
name: Stoic as Hell
prerequisite_markdown: [Chelaxian Scion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=8971); Trained in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); expert in Will

May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny **Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 228 --- You create a faintly glowing sigil (shedding normal light in a 5-foot radius) on the target’s body, after which you command the target to approach you, run away (as if it had the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition), release what it’s holding, drop [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), or stand in place. The target then attempts a Fortitude save against your class DC. **Critical Success** The sigil immediately vanishes, and the creature is unaffected.
**Success** The sigil acts as a magnet for your fiery attacks, granting the creature a –1 penalty to its saves and AC against your fire impulses. The sigil lasts until the end of your next turn or until the creature spends at least 1 action obeying your command, whichever happens first.
**Failure** At the start of the creature’s next turn, if it doesn’t use their first action to obey the command, the sigil ignites as it vanishes, dealing 8d6 fire damage and 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to the creature.
**Critical Failure** The creature must spend the first action of its next turn to obey your command; it can’t [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=2294). The sigil vanishes at the end of its turn; it ignites if the creature didn’t use all its remaining actions to continue to obey your command, dealing 8d6 fire damage and 2d6 persistent fire damage to the creature. --- **Level (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6, and the persistent damage increases by 1d6.
name: Burning Demand

May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny **Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 228 --- Though not unique to Chelaxian kineticists, this [sarglagon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2908)-inspired technique floods enemies’ lungs. Your elemental gate exudes a faint mist that feels as thick as standing water. The moisture doesn’t just tug at limbs; it forcibly seeps into the nostrils and lungs of air-breathing creatures. While in this stance, your aura is difficult terrain for any creature that doesn’t have a swim Speed. Creatures that can’t breathe water must [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) while in the aura, and when such creatures take an action that reduces their remaining air by 1 or more rounds, they must also attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC (maximum once per round). You’re immune to the effects of your own Drowning Mist. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to the effects of this impulse for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** Moisture seeps into the creature’s lungs, making the creature [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but sickened 2.
name: Drowning Mist

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 14"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Liberating Dive](/Feats.aspx?ID=8979) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Composite" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=501" />
<trait label="Impulse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=502" />
<trait label="Kineticist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=504" />
<trait label="Metal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=505" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 229

</column>

---

You sprout wings with steel feathers, resembling those of the [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) [Talmandor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4835). With a powerful flap, the wings propel you across the battlefield, slicing foes and restraints along your path. Until the end of your turn, you gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) equal to your land Speed or 40 feet, whichever is greater. You then [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to your Speed, and this movement doesn’t trigger reactions.

Choose one foe and one ally you’re adjacent to at any point during this movement. Using one wing’s sharpened edge, you can use a 1-action [Elemental Blast](/Actions.aspx?ID=2125) as a melee Strike using the metal element against the foe. With the other wing, you can help the ally break free of an effect [grabbing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [restraining](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [immobilizing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [paralyzing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) them; they either attempt a new save against one such effect that allows a save, or they attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as a free action. The ally can use your [impulse](/Traits.aspx?ID=502) attack modifier in place of their unarmed attack modifier or skill check modifier.
name: Liberating Dive

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 229

</additional-info>

</summary>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Adventurous Outfitter](/Feats.aspx?ID=8980) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 230

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37); [Quick Repair](/Feats.aspx?ID=5198)

</column>

---

You tinker, create, and repair gear as you adventure. You can spend part of your daily preparations adjusting your group’s equipment. This allows you to attempt to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) up to five items during that time.

You gain access to a new exploration activity: Tinker on the Go. So long as you have two free hands and have access to the necessary tools, you can make Crafting progress while out traveling. At the GM’s discretion, you might not be able to Tinker on the Go for projects that require extensive infrastructure, such as a smithing forge. Otherwise, time spent exploring can count toward a day of crafting progress, as long as 8 hours of time get used in this way. 

If you lead a [troop](/Rules.aspx?ID=3439), you maintain their equipment in addition to your companions’ gear. Once per day when you Command the troop in a skirmish encounter, you can increase the DC of their offensive actions by 1 until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Adventurous Outfitter

feat_markdown: As on the Board, so on the Battlefield
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[As on the Board, so on the Battlefield](/Feats.aspx?ID=8981) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=615" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=695" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 231

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41)

</column>

---

You’re a master of strategy games, able to translate strategies and mechanics from these games to the actual battles they were created to simulate. You can use your proficiency rank in [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) for anything that requires proficiency in Warfare Lore (such as prerequisites) and use your Games Lore modifier in place of your Warfare Lore modifier for checks. You can use Games Lore to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on military professionals, such as mercenaries and commanders. Your reputation as a strategist opens up opportunities while you’re in a military camp, near a battlefield, or somewhere else that has numerous non‑hostile soldiers, allowing you to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364) with Games Lore with a task level of at least your level – 1. 

If you [lead a troop](/Rules.aspx?ID=3439) and have had time to create a [skirmish bond](/Rules.aspx?ID=3452) with them, you’ve also spent time going over the finer points of strategy and even played against many members of your troop. Once per day, you can roll Games Lore for initiative when leading this troop.
name: As on the Board, so on the Battlefield

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Hell's Destiny **Source** [Pathfinder #223: Hell's Destiny](/Sources.aspx?ID=336) pg. 231 **Prerequisites** Master in [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) --- You’re a wild card on the battlefield, inventing unpredictable tactics and surprising foes. You employ one of these strategies, adding either your choice of the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait. Attempt a [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check against the [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC of a creature within 30 feet, after which the target becomes temporarily immune to further uses of Improvise Strategy for 1 hour. **Special** Once per day, if you lead a troop that has a [skirmish bond](/Rules.aspx?ID=3452), you can Improvise Strategy to attempt a Warfare Lore check against a standard DC for your troop’s level, rather than targeting a foe. If you succeed, your troop replaces its current skirmish bond with another of your choice until the end of your next turn. **Critical Success** The target gains one of the following conditions of your choice until the end of your next turn: [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1. The target can end the effect by using either a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait to regain their bearings (if your strategy had the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait) or an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to rub their eyes (if your strategy had the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait). In addition, the target isn’t temporarily immune to further uses of Improvise Strategy.
**Success** As critical success, but the target is temporarily immune to further uses of Improvise Strategy.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** The target sees through the gambit, leaving you [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the beginning of your next turn.
name: Improvise Strategy
